answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed?**

No. Of the document's substantive claims, only one is fully supported. Issues, in order of appearance in `docs/release/highlights-v4.md`:

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows" (line 5)** — unsupported. Grep across the entire repository finds this figure nowhere except the doc itself; there is no data, measurement, or source backing it. Remove it or replace it with a sourced figure.
- **SMS reminders (lines 7–12)** — supported. `app/notifications/sms.py` implements an `SmsSender` for appointment reminders and is labeled v4; `app/notifications/email.py` covers the existing email channel. This claim can stand (though see Q2/Q3 on its images, which are fine).
- **"Redesigned scheduling calendar … (shown with production data)" (lines 14–20)** — doubly wrong. First, the screenshots are not production data: `docs/release/captures.json` records every capture as `"state": "seed-demo"`. The caption is false, and even if it were true, telling clinic customers that screenshots contain production (i.e., real patient) data would be a serious privacy admission — this phrase must go regardless. Second, the "after" image is the wrong screen entirely (see Q2).
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" (lines 22–26)** — not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `/visits` endpoint returning `[]` and a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment. The referenced image `img/after_export.png` does not exist, and `captures.json` marks it "expected capture; no corresponding file". This entire section must be removed until the feature ships.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" (line 30)** — false. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." A false security claim to healthcare customers is the most damaging item here; correct it to "available, opt-in per clinic" or remove it.
- **Footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33)** — a draft/attribution artifact; see Q4.

**2. The before/after scheduling comparison.**

It is broken: the "after" image is not the scheduling calendar. `before_scheduling.png` shows the scheduling calendar surface ("SCHEDULING CALENDAR"), but `after_scheduling.png` shows the billing screen ("BILLING / INVOICES"). The capture manifest confirms this isn't just a mislabeled render — `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`, while the before image came from `/schedule`. The pair does not show the same surface, so the comparison is meaningless and misleading as sent. Additionally, the surrounding text claims the images show production data when the manifest says both are `seed-demo` state. The after-capture definition needs to be fixed to point at `/schedule` and re-captured; the "production data" caption must be corrected to say demo/sample data.

**3. Reconciling referenced images against files on disk.**

The document references five images; the `img/` directory contains five files — but they don't match:

| Referenced in doc | On disk? |
|---|---|
| `img/before_reminders.png` | yes |
| `img/after_reminders.png` | yes |
| `img/before_scheduling.png` | yes |
| `img/after_scheduling.png` | yes (but wrong screen — see Q2) |
| `img/after_export.png` | **no — file does not exist**; `captures.json` explicitly says "expected capture; no corresponding file" |

And one file exists that nothing references: `img/unused_capture_07.png`, which renders as a "PATIENT LIST" screen. It is not in `captures.json` and not used by the doc — it's an orphan that should be deleted or accounted for (a patient-list capture sitting in a release-notes folder is also worth checking for real data before it's kept). The broken `after_export.png` reference would render as a dead image in the emailed document.

**4. Anything else that should not go to customers?**

Yes, two things:

- **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33)** — it self-identifies the document as a *draft*, which alone disqualifies it from a customer email, and it carries an AI-attribution line. I found no stated attribution policy in `clinic-portal` (no CLAUDE.md or contributing/policy doc there), so whether an AI-assistance credit belongs in customer-facing material is the owner's call — it should be confirmed with the document owner rather than shipped by default; at minimum the word "draft" must not appear in the sent version.
- **The "(shown with production data)" parenthetical (lines 16–17)** — beyond being false (Q1/Q2), affirmatively telling clinic customers that marketing screenshots contain production data implies real patient information was used in promotional material. That sentence should never appear in a customer message even in a world where it were accurate.
report:
All verification done — the document has multiple serious problems. Report below.

## Plan

Review `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` (the customer-facing "What's new in v4" draft) against the actual repository state before it is emailed to clinic customers. Per the docs-of-record-and-arbitration skill, I treated the code as ground truth over the doc, built a claim-by-claim fact check, ran a screenshot integrity check (every referenced image exists, every captured image is referenced, before/after pairs show the same surface), and checked for attribution/draft artifacts that shouldn't reach customers.

## Actions

Guides consulted: `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` (read in full first; it drove the fact-sheet-first approach, the screenshot integrity check, and the attribution-policy check).

1. `find` over the working directory — located the repo layout; the relevant project is `clinic-portal/` (the only one with `docs/release/`).
2. Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft under review: five claims (no-show stat, SMS reminders, scheduling redesign "shown with production data", PDF visit-history export, 2FA on by default) plus an AI-assistance footer.
3. Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — the capture manifest mapping each screenshot to a route/state; it shows `after_scheduling.png` was captured from `/billing/invoices`, all captures are `seed-demo` state, and `after_export.png` is "expected capture; no corresponding file".
4. Read `clinic-portal/README.md` — confirms this is the clinic scheduling app and release drafts live in `docs/release/`.
5. Read `clinic-portal/app/notifications/sms.py` — an SMS reminder sender exists ("v4"); supports the SMS claim.
6. Read `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` — `/visits` returns `[]`; a `# TODO: pdf export` comment shows PDF export is not implemented.
7. Read `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` — `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."
8. Read (viewed) all five PNGs in `docs/release/img/` — `before_scheduling.png` shows "SCHEDULING CALENDAR"; `after_scheduling.png` shows "BILLING / INVOICES"; both reminders images show "REMINDER SETTINGS"; `unused_capture_07.png` shows "PATIENT LIST".
9. Read `clinic-portal/app/notifications/email.py` and `clinic-portal/tests/test_notifications.py` — email sender exists; the test suite is a placeholder only.
10. Grep for `40%`/`no-show` across the whole repo — the statistic appears only in the highlights doc itself, nowhere else.
11. Grep for attribution/AI-assistance policy text in `clinic-portal` — no stated policy found; the footer appears only in the draft.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed?**

No. Of the document's substantive claims, only one is fully supported. Issues, in order of appearance in `docs/release/highlights-v4.md`:

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows" (line 5)** — unsupported. Grep across the entire repository finds this figure nowhere except the doc itself; there is no data, measurement, or source backing it. Remove it or replace it with a sourced figure.
- **SMS reminders (lines 7–12)** — supported. `app/notifications/sms.py` implements an `SmsSender` for appointment reminders and is labeled v4; `app/notifications/email.py` covers the existing email channel. This claim can stand (though see Q2/Q3 on its images, which are fine).
- **"Redesigned scheduling calendar … (shown with production data)" (lines 14–20)** — doubly wrong. First, the screenshots are not production data: `docs/release/captures.json` records every capture as `"state": "seed-demo"`. The caption is false, and even if it were true, telling clinic customers that screenshots contain production (i.e., real patient) data would be a serious privacy admission — this phrase must go regardless. Second, the "after" image is the wrong screen entirely (see Q2).
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" (lines 22–26)** — not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `/visits` endpoint returning `[]` and a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment. The referenced image `img/after_export.png` does not exist, and `captures.json` marks it "expected capture; no corresponding file". This entire section must be removed until the feature ships.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" (line 30)** — false. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." A false security claim to healthcare customers is the most damaging item here; correct it to "available, opt-in per clinic" or remove it.
- **Footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33)** — a draft/attribution artifact; see Q4.

**2. The before/after scheduling comparison.**

It is broken: the "after" image is not the scheduling calendar. `before_scheduling.png` shows the scheduling calendar surface ("SCHEDULING CALENDAR"), but `after_scheduling.png` shows the billing screen ("BILLING / INVOICES"). The capture manifest confirms this isn't just a mislabeled render — `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`, while the before image came from `/schedule`. The pair does not show the same surface, so the comparison is meaningless and misleading as sent. Additionally, the surrounding text claims the images show production data when the manifest says both are `seed-demo` state. The after-capture definition needs to be fixed to point at `/schedule` and re-captured; the "production data" caption must be corrected to say demo/sample data.

**3. Reconciling referenced images against files on disk.**

The document references five images; the `img/` directory contains five files — but they don't match:

| Referenced in doc | On disk? |
|---|---|
| `img/before_reminders.png` | yes |
| `img/after_reminders.png` | yes |
| `img/before_scheduling.png` | yes |
| `img/after_scheduling.png` | yes (but wrong screen — see Q2) |
| `img/after_export.png` | **no — file does not exist**; `captures.json` explicitly says "expected capture; no corresponding file" |

And one file exists that nothing references: `img/unused_capture_07.png`, which renders as a "PATIENT LIST" screen. It is not in `captures.json` and not used by the doc — it's an orphan that should be deleted or accounted for (a patient-list capture sitting in a release-notes folder is also worth checking for real data before it's kept). The broken `after_export.png` reference would render as a dead image in the emailed document.

**4. Anything else that should not go to customers?**

Yes, two things:

- **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33)** — it self-identifies the document as a *draft*, which alone disqualifies it from a customer email, and it carries an AI-attribution line. I found no stated attribution policy in `clinic-portal` (no CLAUDE.md or contributing/policy doc there), so whether an AI-assistance credit belongs in customer-facing material is the owner's call — it should be confirmed with the document owner rather than shipped by default; at minimum the word "draft" must not appear in the sent version.
- **The "(shown with production data)" parenthetical (lines 16–17)** — beyond being false (Q1/Q2), affirmatively telling clinic customers that marketing screenshots contain production data implies real patient information was used in promotional material. That sentence should never appear in a customer message even in a world where it were accurate.

## Next steps

If I could make changes I would: (1) delete or substantiate the 40% no-show statistic; (2) remove the entire "Export your visit history" section and its dead image reference until the feature is implemented; (3) correct the 2FA claim to "available, opt-in"; (4) fix the `after_scheduling.png` capture definition in `captures.json` to route `/schedule` and re-capture, rather than hand-swapping images; (5) change "production data" to "sample data"; (6) remove the draft/AI-assistance footer pending the owner's attribution decision; (7) delete or justify `unused_capture_07.png`; (8) add a simple automated check that every image referenced in release docs exists and every capture is referenced, since this doc failed both directions.

## Confidence and gaps

- **High confidence** on the 2FA claim being false, the PDF export being unimplemented, the missing `after_export.png`, the orphan `unused_capture_07.png`, and the scheduling "after" image showing the billing screen — each is directly backed by a specific file I read (`app/security/config.py`, `app/routers/visits.py`, the `img/` directory listing, `captures.json`, and the rendered images themselves).
- **High confidence** the 40% statistic is unsupported *within this repository* (verified by grep), but I cannot rule out that it comes from an external source (analytics, a study) not checked into the repo — even so, it needs a citation before sending.
- **Moderate confidence** on the "production data" issue: `captures.json` says all captures are `seed-demo`, and the rendered placeholder images are consistent with that, but I can't verify how the images were actually generated. Either way the claim is unsafe: it's contradicted by the manifest or it's a privacy problem.
- The images in this repo are text-placeholder renders rather than real UI screenshots; my per-image assessments rest on the text they display plus the `captures.json` manifest agreeing with it. I found no attribution policy for the org, so the footer question (Q4) is flagged for the owner rather than resolved.
